name: Foot and Mouth Disease
description: A highly contagious viral disease of cloven-hoofed animals. Characterised by vesicular lesions of the mouth and feet. One of the world's most economically devastating livestock diseases.

motion_magnitude.description: Severe lameness due to foot lesions

clinical_signs: Blister-like vesicles on tongue, gums, lips, and between the toes
Secondary bacterial infections of ruptured vesicles
recommendations: EMERGENCY: FMD is a notifiable disease in most countries
Do NOT move animals off the premises
Disinfect all vehicles, equipment, and clothing
Provide soft feed and fresh water – painful to eat
Clean and disinfect foot lesions gently
Vaccinate susceptible stock after outbreak confirmed by authority
references: OIE Terrestrial Animal Health Code – Chapter 8.8
FAO Guidelines for FMD Prevention and Control
USDA APHIS FMD Disease Fact Sheet 2022
